records.ety: [Cf. D. bobbel, Dan. boble, Sw. bubbla. Cf. Blob, n.]

defs: A thin film of liquid inflated with air or gas; as, a soap bubble; bubbles on the surface of a river.
quotes: Beads of sweat have stood upon thy brow, Like bubbles in a late disturbed stream.

defs: A small quantity of air or gas within a liquid body; as, bubbles rising in champagne or aërated waters.
A globule of air, or globular vacuum, in a transparent solid; as, bubbles in window glass, or in a lens.
A small, hollow, floating bead or globe, formerly used for testing the strength of spirits.
The globule of air in the spirit tube of a level.

Anything that wants firmness or solidity; that which is more specious than real; a false show; a cheat or fraud; a delusive scheme; an empty project; a dishonest speculation; as, the South Sea bubble.
quotes: Then a soldier . . . Seeking the bubble reputation Even in the cannon's mouth.

defs: A person deceived by an empty project; a gull.

records.ety: [Cf. D. bobbelen, Dan. boble. See Bubble, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Bubbled (<?/); p. pr. & vb. n. Bubbling (<?/).]

defs: To rise in bubbles, as liquids when boiling or agitated; to contain bubbles.
quotes: The milk that bubbled in the pail.

defs: To run with a gurgling noise, as if forming bubbles; as, a bubbling stream.
To sing with a gurgling or warbling sound.
quotes: At mine ear Bubbled the nightingale and heeded not.
